evals.name: sense-split-vs-lump
evals.prompt: I'm building a Yoruba word-sense-disambiguation eval set. Should I use granular senses (every nuance gets its own entry) or lumped (broad senses)?
evals.expected_output: Should: (1) corpus-design decision based on use case; (2) granular for fine-grained WSD eval; (3) lumped for easier inter-annotator agreement; (4) cite Atkins & Rundell for methodology; (5) explicit policy MUST be documented; (6) NEVER mix conventions within same lexicon.

evals.name: mwe-glossary-rag
evals.prompt: We're building a RAG system for Hindi technical documents. Should we extract MWEs into a glossary?
evals.expected_output: Should: (1) YES — MWE / technical-term inventory drives glossary injection; (2) literal mistranslation of MWEs is dominant failure mode; (3) PARSEME Hindi data exists; (4) cross-reference linguistic-semantics MWE handling; (5) variant handling for spelling / dialectal variants; (6) note technical vocabulary often pre-reform spellings.

evals.name: wiktionary-as-gold-trap
evals.prompt: Wiktionary has Arabic dictionary entries. Can I treat them as gold for production?
evals.expected_output: Should: (1) NO — Wiktionary quality varies wildly; useful as starting source, not production gold; (2) recommend curator pass + native-speaker review; (3) DBnary as structured wrap; (4) PanLex bridges many bilingual dictionaries; (5) Arabic-specific: per-dialect coverage gaps; (6) ethics + community partnership for serious work.
